license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.ygvftw6
title: Histological staining of fish gonadal tissue
author: Jhennifer Gomes Cordeiro

steps_raw.instruction: Anesthetize and euthanize the animals

Perform a ventro-longitudinal incision to expose the gonads, Bouin's fixative must be immediately applied on the gonads aiming the prefixation. Then, gonads are removed and immediately placed in the fixative
2Leave gonads overnight in Bouin's fixative for 24h 24:00:00
After 24h, the fixative solution must be replaced with 70% ethanol, that should be renewed every day until there is no trace of fixative
Before dehydration the tissue must be sectioned in the desired shape and fit into the molds
Samples need to be placed in glass vials, to which the solutions will be added
Transfer the samples to three fast changes of 70%, 80%, and 90% ethanol 00:03:00, for each change
Leave the samples at 95% ethanol for 40 min. 00:40:00
Include samples in 1:1 solution of 95% alcohol and infiltration resin (Methacrylate glycol) for 40 minutes 00:40:00
Leave samples in infiltration resin (methacrylate glycol) "overnight" (or at least 12 hours) 12:00:00
Prepare the inclusion resin solution (Methacrylate glycol) with the hardener (for each 1.0 mL of resin use 0.07 mL of hardener)
Embedding the material: place the material on the mold and place the resin + hardener solution on it by completing the mold
After the resin polymerizes, leave embedded samples in a drying stove for 24 h 24:00:00
Unblock and glue on microtome support stick with epoxy resin glue
After the glue has completely dried, the sample should be taken to the microtome
The sample should be sectioned to the thickness of 3 to 5 μm
The cuts should be deposited on properly identified slides

The slides should be kept in the drying stove for 1 day and then followed for staining procedures. If staining with H&E, follow steps 19-27. If staining with toluidine blue, follow steps 28-33 24:00:00
Moisturize cuts in running tap water for 10 minutes 00:10:00
Leave in Harri's hematoxylin solution for 15 minutes 00:15:00
1Wash in running tap water for 10 minutes 00:10:00
Dehydrate in 90% alcohol with fast change (if eosin is alcoholic) 00:03:00
Leave in eosin solution for 15 minutes 00:15:00
Rinse well under running water

Transfer the samples to three fast changes of 70%, 80%, and 95% ethanol, followed by fast passes in absolute ethanol, and two changes of xylene (3 min for each station) for clearing before coverslipping 00:03:00, for each change
Transfer the sample to a third change of xylene 00:03:00
Mount and coverslip in Permount (xylene-based mounting medium)
Moisturize cuts in running water for 10 minutes 00:10:00
Leave in toluidine blue solution for 15 minutes 00:15:00
Wash in running water for 10 minutes 00:10:00

Transfer the samples to three fast passes of 70%, 80%, and 95% ethanol, followed by fast passes in absolute ethanol, and two changes of xylene (3 min for each station) for clearing before coverslipping 00:03:00, for each change
Transfer the sample to a third change of xylene 00:03:00
Mount and coverslip in Permount (xylene-based mounting medium)
Check slides on the microscope and take photographs as needed. Example slides: Immature female Astyanax bimaculatus gonads Initial gonadal development in male Astyanax bimaculatus
